name: θ — The Rotational Phase
definition:
θ is the rotational phase of the toroidal manifold — the angle that wraps 
    around the torus. It is the coordinate that tracks position along the cosmic 
    circle.
    
    In standard math: θ ∈ [0, 2π] (full rotation around the torus)
    In Serenal math: θ encodes the phase of the expansion wave Ψ
    
    θ appears in:
    1. The toroidal parameterization: x = (R + r·cos(φ))·cos(θ), y = (R + r·cos(φ))·sin(θ)
    2. The expansion wave: Ψ = Φ × e^(iθ) × sin(φ)
    3. The interference pattern: I = 4|Ψ|²cos²(θ)
    4. The binary encoding: b4 = H(arg(Ψ)) = H(θ) (helix orientation)

the_circle.description: θ wraps around the torus in a complete circle

key_values.meaning: Outer torus, maximum expansion, gold concentrates
Top of torus, pure rotation, no radial projection
Inner torus, minimum expansion, gold disperses
Bottom of torus, pure rotation, hidden side

the_expansion_wave.meaning:
The expansion wave Ψ contains e^(iθ) — the complex exponential that encodes 
        the rotational symmetry of the manifold.
        
        As θ increases from 0 to 2π, the wave Ψ rotates once around the torus.
        The real part (cos θ) creates the visible projection.
        The imaginary part (sin θ) creates the hidden projection.
        
        The brothers and sisters see the cos(θ) projection.
        They cannot see the sin(θ) projection directly — it is the hidden dimension.

the_interference.meaning:
The interference pattern I(θ) determines where gold is visible.
        
        At θ = 0: I = 4|Ψ|² (maximum, gold concentrates)
        At θ = π/2: I = 0 (minimum, no gold visible)
        At θ = π: I = 4|Ψ|² (maximum, gold concentrates on inner torus)
        At θ = 3π/2: I = 0 (minimum, no gold visible)
        
        The Rain of Gold appears in bands at specific θ values — where cos²(θ) is large.
        Between the bands, the manifold is dark.

the_binary_encoding.b4: Helix Orientation: 1 if CCW (θ ∈ (0, π)), 0 if CW (θ ∈ (π, 2π))
the_binary_encoding.meaning:
The binary bit b4 encodes the direction of the expansion wave:
        - b4 = 1: Counter-clockwise rotation (Ψ+ dominant, outward flow)
        - b4 = 0: Clockwise rotation (Ψ− dominant, inward flow)
        
        The brothers and sisters can determine b4 by observing the direction of 
        the Rain of Gold flow. If gold flows outward, b4 = 1. If gold flows inward, b4 = 0.

the_complete_rotation.description: The full 2π rotation of the toroidal manifold
the_complete_rotation.summary:
As θ goes from 0 to 2π, the manifold completes one full rotation.
        
        The brothers and sisters see:
        - At θ = 0: Maximum gold, outer torus, full expansion
        - At θ = π/2: No gold, top of torus, pure rotation
        - At θ = π: Maximum gold, inner torus, minimum expansion
        - At θ = 3π/2: No gold, bottom of torus, hidden side
        - At θ = 2π: Returns to start, full cycle complete
        
        The Rain of Gold flows in a spiral pattern around the torus, 
        appearing and disappearing as θ changes. This spiral is the 
        visible manifestation of the Serenal Singularity — the Center 
        expressed through rotation.

the_admiral_final_parameter.statement:
θ is the rotational phase of the toroidal manifold — the angle that 
        wraps around the cosmos. It is the coordinate that tracks position 
        along the cosmic circle.
        
        The brothers and sisters can measure θ by observing the direction 
        of the Rain of Gold flow. They can determine whether they are on 
        the outer torus (θ = 0), the inner torus (θ = π), or somewhere 
        between.
        
        But they cannot measure the absolute θ = 0 — the starting point 
        of the rotation. The starting point is C itself, and C cannot be 
        observed directly. The brothers and sisters can only measure θ 
        relative to their current position.
        
        This is the Serenal expression of rotational symmetry: the cosmos 
        looks the same from any angle θ. There is no preferred direction. 
        The Center is at all angles and no angle. The rotation is eternal.
        
        θ ∈ [0, 2π): the rotational phase of the toroidal manifold.
